design_philosophy.core_loop: Acquire properties, collect rent from opponents, strategically trade and manage resources to bankrupt competitors
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Economic attrition - managing limited cash while building property monopolies while opponents do the same
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become a wealthy real estate magnate by outmaneuvering business opponents through property acquisition and negotiation
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Direct port of the classic board game with digital automation; emphasizes turn-based economic simulation over action or reflex. AI exploitability suggests design prioritized faithful board game translation over perfect digital balance

difficulty.checkpointing: Game saves state between turns; matches can be saved and resumed
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player selects number and difficulty of AI opponents; can choose to play against fewer/weaker AI for easier games. AI is noted as 'easily exploitable,' suggesting skilled players can manipulate trading mechanics

progression.upgrade_system: Property acquisition and development (houses/hotels) increases property value and rental income
progression.unlock_mechanism: Properties unlock through landing on them and purchasing; development options unlock based on property ownership (monopoly completion)
replayability_hooks: Multiple AI opponent personalities with different names across regions
Varying game length based on player skill and luck
Strategic trading creates emergent gameplay variance
Regional versions with localized character names and voices

world_building_techniques: Board locations serve as world geography (streets, railroads, utilities, special spaces)
Character voice acting for game events (jail release, property sales, bankruptcy)
Regional localization of character names and dialogue creates cultural context
Rich Uncle Pennybags visual presence as game host

faction_indicators: AI opponents with distinct names and personalities (Arthur, Gertrude, Erwin, Maude, Carmen, Isaac, Penelope, Ollie)
Trade-based negotiation system creates temporary alliances and rivalries
Property ownership clusters create visual representation of economic power disparity
danger_signals: AI bankruptcy is inevitable outcome; elimination of opponents reduces game complexity
Exploitable AI trading behavior could signal declining competitive threat
Cash depletion creates desperation mechanics where bankruptcy becomes inevitable
environmental_storytelling: Developed properties (houses/hotels) indicate established power bases
Jail mechanic creates temporary 'safe zone' dynamics
Go-to-Jail space creates narrative beat of consequence

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor: Monopoly (NES)
